--- title: "Interactive dashboard" subtitle: "Slide the boundary along the abundance distribution and rebuild the results" filters: - shinylive format: reefcode-html: embed-resources: false --- > Olinger LK, Edmunds PJ, Levitan D, Smith TB, Lasker H, Feeley M, Mahoney L, Dahl A. Reproductive mode, but not rarity, influences population trajectories in corals. In Review 2026. The rarity result rests on one choice: the boundary that separates common taxa from rare taxa along the ranked abundance distribution. The manuscript sets it where the top taxa reach 90 percent of baseline cover. This tool exposes that choice. At the paper's boundary the tool reproduces the published results. ## The live tool (runs in your browser) The app below runs entirely in your browser through shinylive (WebAssembly R). The first load fetches the R runtime, so give it a moment. Every partition is pre-solved, so moving a boundary swaps stored results instead of fitting a model. ```{shinylive-r} #| standalone: true #| viewerHeight: 1600 ## file: app.R # webR-safe (shinylive) version of the rare/common boundary explorer. # Uses only base R + shiny + dplyr (no ggplot2, no flextable): ggplot2's dependency chain # (munsell/scales/...) does not resolve in webR, and flextable/officer do not run there. # The plots are drawn in base graphics and Table 1 is hand-built HTML. Every partition is # pre-solved (bundled in pre_b64.txt), so nothing is fitted at run time. library(shiny) library(dplyr) b64 <- paste(readLines("pre_b64.txt"), collapse = "") pre <- unserialize(memDecompress(jsonlite::base64_dec(b64), "gzip")) progs <- c("TCRMP", "VINPS", "CSUN") palette_grp <- c("Common.Broadcaster" = "#0072B2", "Rare.Broadcaster" = "#56B4E9", "Common.Brooder" = "#D55E00", "Rare.Brooder" = "#E69F00") label_grp <- c("Common.Broadcaster" = "Common broadcaster", "Rare.Broadcaster" = "Rare broadcaster", "Common.Brooder" = "Common brooder", "Rare.Brooder" = "Rare brooder") term_labels <- c("(Intercept)" = "Intercept", "yearind" = "Year", "categoryRare" = "Category (Rare)", "Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Repro. mode (Brooder)", "yearind:categoryRare" = "Year x Category", "yearind:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Year x Repro. mode", "categoryRare:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Category x Repro. mode", "yearind:categoryRare:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Year x Cat x Repro") # --- base-R plots ----------------------------------------------------------- plot_sad <- function(prog, part) { d <- part$classification ord <- order(prog$baseline$rank[match(d$taxon, prog$baseline$taxon)]) d <- d[ord, ] # most abundant first grp <- paste(d$commonness, d$Reproductive_mode, sep = ".") cols <- palette_grp[grp]; cols[is.na(cols)] <- "grey70" op <- par(mar = c(4, 11, 3, 1)); on.exit(par(op)) bp <- barplot(rev(d$meancov), horiz = TRUE, col = rev(cols), border = NA, names.arg = rev(d$taxon), las = 1, cex.names = 0.72, xlab = "Mean baseline abundance", main = sprintf("%d common / %d rare", part$n_common, prog$N - part$n_common)) nb <- nrow(d) if (part$n_common >= 1 && part$n_common < nb) # boundary between common and rare bars abline(h = mean(bp[c(nb - part$n_common, nb - part$n_common + 1)]), lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "grey10") } plot_trend <- function(part) { d <- part$analysis grp <- paste(d$category, d$Reproductive_mode, sep = ".") cols <- palette_grp[grp] op <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 1, 1)); on.exit(par(op)) plot(d$year, d$perccover, log = "y", pch = 21, cex = 1.1, bg = adjustcolor(cols, 0.35), col = "#00000055", xlab = "Year", ylab = "Abundance (log scale)") for (g in unique(grp)) { sub <- d[grp == g, ] if (nrow(sub) > 2) { m <- lm(log10(perccover) ~ year, sub) xs <- range(sub$year); ys <- 10^predict(m, data.frame(year = xs)) lines(xs, ys, col = palette_grp[g], lwd = 2.5) } } legend("topright", legend = label_grp[names(palette_grp)], col = palette_grp, lwd = 2.5, cex = 0.85, bty = "n") } part_of <- function(p, k) { pp <- pre[[p]] key <- as.character(min(max(round(k), min(pp$valid_k)), max(pp$valid_k))) pp$parts[[key]] } ui <- fluidPage( tags$style(HTML( "body{font-family:'Space Grotesk',system-ui,sans-serif} .pubtbl{border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13px;margin:6px 0 4px} .pubtbl th,.pubtbl td{border-bottom:1px solid #ddd6ca;padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;vertical-align:top} .pubtbl td:first-child,.pubtbl th:first-child{text-align:left} .pubtbl thead th{background:#0b2444;color:#fff;white-space:pre-line;font-weight:600}")), h4("Rare / common boundary explorer"), p("Move each program's boundary. Taxa above the split are common, below are rare. The abundance ", "distribution, the temporal trends, and Table 1 all rebuild together. Each slider starts at the ", "paper's 0.90 boundary."), actionButton("reset", "Reset all to the paper's boundary (0.90)"), tags$hr(), lapply(progs, function(p) tagList( h5(sprintf("%s (%s)", p, pre[[p]]$taxon_level)), uiOutput(paste0("sl_", p)), fluidRow(column(5, plotOutput(paste0("sad_", p), height = "400px")), column(7, plotOutput(paste0("trend_", p), height = "400px"))), tags$hr() )), h5("Table 1, regenerated at the current boundaries"), uiOutput("table1"), br(), h5("Supplemental: group-specific temporal slopes"), tableOutput("slopes") ) server <- function(input, output, session) { po <- function(p) part_of(p, input[[paste0("k_", p)]]) lapply(progs, function(p) { output[[paste0("sl_", p)]] <- renderUI({ pp <- pre[[p]] sliderInput(paste0("k_", p), sprintf("common taxa (%s)", p), min = min(pp$valid_k), max = max(pp$valid_k), value = pp$k_paper, step = 1, width = "100%") }) output[[paste0("sad_", p)]] <- renderPlot({ req(input[[paste0("k_", p)]]); plot_sad(pre[[p]], po(p)) }) output[[paste0("trend_", p)]] <- renderPlot({ req(input[[paste0("k_", p)]]); plot_trend(po(p)) }) }) observeEvent(input$reset, { for (p in progs) updateSliderInput(session, paste0("k_", p), value = pre[[p]]$k_paper) }) output$table1 <- renderUI({ req(input$k_TCRMP, input$k_VINPS, input$k_CSUN) parts <- lapply(progs, po); req(all(!vapply(parts, is.null, logical(1)))) f2 <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 2) cell <- function(est, se, p) { st <- ifelse(p < 0.001, "***", ifelse(p < 0.01, "**", ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", ""))) pt <- ifelse(p < 0.001, "p<0.001", paste0("p=", formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3))) HTML(paste0(f2(est), " (", f2(se), ")
", pt, st)) } co1 <- parts[[1]]$coef body <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(co1)), function(i) tags$tr(tags$td(term_labels[[co1$term[i]]]), lapply(seq_along(progs), function(j) { co <- parts[[j]]$coef; sig <- co$p.value[i] < 0.05 tags$td(style = if (sig) "font-weight:700" else "", cell(co$estimate[i], co$std.error[i], co$p.value[i])) }))) stat_labs <- c("Adj. R-squared", "Observations", "Residual SE", "df") stat_vals <- lapply(parts, function(pt) { fs <- pt$fit_stats c(formatC(fs$adj_r2, format = "f", digits = 3), fs$n, formatC(fs$rse, format = "f", digits = 3), fs$df) }) stats <- lapply(seq_along(stat_labs), function(r) tags$tr(tags$td(stat_labs[r]), lapply(stat_vals, function(v) tags$td(v[r])))) tags$div( tags$table(class = "pubtbl", tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Predictor"), lapply(progs, function(p) tags$th(paste0(p, "\nEstimate (SE)\nP-value"))))), tags$tbody(body, stats)), tags$p(style = "font-size:12px;color:#6d685e", "Table 1. Linear-model coefficients with HC3 robust standard errors. Dependent variable: ", "log10 percent cover. Significant terms (p<0.05) in bold.")) }) output$slopes <- renderTable({ req(input$k_TCRMP, input$k_VINPS, input$k_CSUN) do.call(rbind, lapply(progs, function(p) { po(p)$slopes %>% transmute(Program = p, Group, Slope = sprintf("%.4f", Slope), SE = sprintf("%.4f", SE), `Annual % change` = sprintf("%.2f", `Annual % change`), `P-value` = ifelse(`P-value` < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", `P-value`))) })) }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, width = "100%") } shinyApp(ui, server) ## file: pre_b64.txt eJzsXQdcE+ffB0VAcYCCe+Be1eK2dXyjuHfFWWcMAaIhwSRQse6997YO3HshijjYe28EB0MQ The comparison below shows the effect without the app. For TCRMP, it moves the boundary to three positions and reports the group slopes. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-summary="Show code: TCRMP group slopes at three boundary positions"} ks <- c(5, pre$TCRMP$k_paper, 20) do.call(rbind, lapply(ks, function(k) { pt <- pre$TCRMP$parts[[as.character(k)]] pt$slopes |> transmute(`Common taxa` = k, `Cumulative cover` = sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * pt$cum_cover), Group, `Annual % change` = sprintf("%.2f", `Annual % change`), `P-value` = ifelse(`P-value` < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", `P-value`))) })) |> kbl(align = c("r", "r", "l", "r", "r"), caption = "TCRMP group slopes at three boundaries. The middle block is the paper's 0.90 boundary.") |> kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |> collapse_rows(columns = 1:2, valign = "top") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: 'xfun::attr()' is deprecated. Use 'xfun::attr2()' instead. See help("Deprecated") ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
TCRMP group slopes at three boundaries. The middle block is the paper's 0.90 boundary.
Common taxa Cumulative cover Group Annual % change P-value
5 67% Common broadcaster -5.39 < 0.001
Rare broadcaster -4.16 < 0.001
Common brooder -1.36 0.035
Rare brooder -2.87 < 0.001
10 90% Common broadcaster -4.26 < 0.001
Rare broadcaster -5.75 < 0.001
Common brooder -1.90 0.002
Rare brooder -1.31 0.131
20 97% Common broadcaster -4.64 < 0.001
Rare broadcaster -0.19 0.791
Common brooder -2.19 < 0.001
Rare brooder -0.41 0.631
````` ::: ::: The classification changes, and the slopes move with it, but the ranking of the four groups by rate of decline stays stable.
